---
title: "model comparison"
author: "Maddee Wiggins (FlowWest)"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Comparison of Results

*The following compares models and results for the FlowWest Chinook and Steelhead models to the FERC 2004 report that analyzed the same data.*

### FlowWest Models:

**Goal**: model that reflects the significance of cover, substrate, depth, and velocity on steelhead presence and absence

-   binary logistic regression

-   response variable - presence/absence

-   mixed effect framework

-   retains full ecological covariate set

### FERC 2004 Model

Report: [SP F-10 3A FINAL REPORT DISTRIBUTION AND HABITAT USE OF JUVENILE STEELHEAD AND OTHER FISHES OF THE LOWER FEATHER RIVER](https://netorg629193.sharepoint.com/sites/VA-FeatherRiver/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FVA%2DFeatherRiver%2FShared%20Documents%2FResources%2FFeather%20River%20Reports%2F04%2D28%2D04%5Fatt%5F10%5Ff10%5F3A%5Fsteelhead%5Fhab%5Fuse%2Epdf&parent=%2Fsites%2FVA%2DFeatherRiver%2FShared%20Documents%2FResources%2FFeather%20River%20Reports&p=true&ga=1)

**Goal:** A habitat-use inference model to guide management and restoration planning

-   Binary logistic regression with fixed-effect

-   no random effects

-   AIC-based variable selection

-   model captures habitat quality

### Differences

| Variable | FERC 2004 Report Findings | Chinook Model | Steelhead Model |
|----|----|----|----|
| **Depth** | Steelhead associated with shallower margins; Chinook vary by size class | Positive and highly significant effect on presence | Negative and significant effect on presence |
| **Velocity** | Steelhead avoid higher velocities; prefer lower velocity microhabitats | Strong negative effect on presence | Strong negative effect on presence (larger magnitude than Chinook) |
| **Substrate** | Gravel/cobble more frequently used than fine substrates | Cobble and boulder not significant once hydraulics included | Substrate not significant once hydraulics included |
| **In-stream Cover** | Positive association with instream cover; Large cover important for some size classes | Large woody not significant; small woody positive but moderate | Small woody positive and significant |
| **Overhanging Vegetation** | Large cover important for some size classes | Strong positive and significant effect | Strong positive and significant effect |
| **Aquatic Vegetation** | Positive association in low-velocity areas | Negative and significant | Positive and significant |
| **Undercut Bank** | positive association reported | Positive, not significant | Positive, not significant |
| **Spawning (Redd) Signal** | Not included in models | Redd total significant; redd presence marginal | Redd presence strong; redd total significant |
| **Temporal Structure** | Seasonal patterns described but not modeled hierarchically | Strong month variance component | Weak month variance component |

### Model Evaluation

*How are models evaluated for performance? Are they?*

-   **FlowWest models**

    -   Goal: Are certain habitat variables significantly associated with fish presence? How well does the model predict presence and how robust is the prediction

    -   Evaluation: AUC, confusion matrix, p-values

-   **2004 FERC report**

    -   Goal: Are certain habitat variables significantly associated with fish presence?

    -   Evaluation: P-values, AIC

-   [**Favrot et al.**](https://netorg629193.sharepoint.com/sites/VA-FeatherRiver/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FVA%2DFeatherRiver%2FShared%20Documents%2FSystematic%20Lit%20Review%2FJune%2012%20%2D%20Lucy%20Lit%20Pull%2Ffavrot%5Fet%5Fal%5F2018%2Epdf&parent=%2Fsites%2FVA%2DFeatherRiver%2FShared%20Documents%2FSystematic%20Lit%20Review%2FJune%2012%20%2D%20Lucy%20Lit%20Pull&p=true&ga=1)**,**

    -   Goal: Is this the best-supported explanatory model for habitat selection?

    -   Evaluation: AIC-based model selection with mixed-effects RSF, residual diagnostics, confidence intervals/odds ratios, and Bayesian credible intervals

-   [Beakes et al., 2012](https://netorg629193.sharepoint.com/sites/VA-FeatherRiver/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?viewid=63d6df4b%2Dcf55%2D4d2a%2Dbcf2%2D1ebdd8dc0dc3&as=json&id=%2Fsites%2FVA%2DFeatherRiver%2FShared%20Documents%2FResources%2FLiterature%2FBeakes%5Fetal%5FRRA%5Finpress%20%281%29%2Epdf&parent=%2Fsites%2FVA%2DFeatherRiver%2FShared%20Documents%2FResources%2FLiterature)

    -   Goal: compare two statistical approaches for modeling juvenile Chinook salmon habitat - first model is a traditional composite suitability index style aggregated univariate model, second model is a multivariate GLM using AIC model selection

    -   Evaluation: Cohen's Kappa, optimized threshold, percent correctly classified, sensitivity, specificity, AUC

        | Metric      | Beakes Model G | FW Steelhead | FW Chinook |
        |-------------|----------------|--------------|------------|
        | AUC         | 0.649          | 0.913        | 0.907      |
        | Kappa       | 0.203          | 0.31         | 0.23       |
        | Sensitivity | 37.5%          | 27%          | 14.1%      |
        | Specificity | 83.6%          | 99.5%        | 99.8%      |

-   *Kappa - a measure of all possible outcomes of presence or absence that are predicted correctly, after accounting for chance predictions*

-   *Sensitivity - Measures how well the model detects fish when they are present*

-   *Specificity - Measures how well the model avoids false alarms.*

-   *AUC - Measures how well a model can distinguish between presence and absence across all possible probability thresholds, not just one cutoff*

```{r eval=FALSE, include=FALSE}
# for reference, FlowWest models:
# Steelhead κ ≈ 0.31
# Chinook κ ≈ 0.23
```

### Figures Comparing FlowWest Chinook and Steelhead Models

**Conallin et al. 2014 - Figure 5**

![](images/clipboard-3948173119.png){width="75%"}

![](images/clipboard-1309889640.png){width="75%"}
